formula.label: Visual AI condition
formula.body: Technician mobile photos are checked for yellowing, wilting, bare patches, uneven growth, pest/disease marks and overall fullness.
formula.proof: Standard-angle photo set, asset ID, timestamp and AI finding record.

formula.label: Sensor stability
formula.body: Water level, moisture, light, temperature, humidity, nutrient signal and gateway heartbeat reduce or support the score.
formula.proof: Telemetry reading, target band, last-seen time and alert status.

formula.label: Service compliance
formula.body: On-time visits, completed care tasks, replacement closure and unresolved work orders affect operational health.
formula.proof: Work order status, technician note, photo proof and audit event.

formula.label: Recovery trend
formula.body: Survival rate, repeated issue history, replacement cycle and recovery speed show whether a wall is improving or degrading.
formula.proof: Historical health score, survival rate, issue recurrence and replacement log.

sources.label: Mobile photo capture
sources.body: Technicians capture fixed-angle photos during visits. AI reads plant condition, while humans approve exceptions before client reporting.

sources.label: Environmental telemetry
sources.body: Water, light, temperature, humidity and gateway data explain why visual health changes and trigger early intervention.

sources.label: Work order ledger
sources.body: Inspection cadence, open issues, completion proof and replacement records anchor the score in actual FM delivery.

sources.label: Manual review
sources.body: FM leads can confirm, override or request retake when lighting, angle or client context makes AI confidence weak.

captureWorkflow.body: Technician scans QR or selects the asset ID, so every photo is tied to a client, site, wall and zone.
The app asks for full-wall, zone close-up and issue photos using fixed prompts for angle, distance and lighting.
AI checks image quality and plant condition, then highlights yellowing, sparse zones, wilt, low-light symptoms and likely causes.
FM lead approves the health score, asks for a retake or converts findings into work orders before the client sees the report.

cameraRoadmap.label: Mobile phone camera
cameraRoadmap.body: Lowest-cost and privacy-safe path. No built-in wall camera is required for launch.

cameraRoadmap.label: Optional fixed plant camera
cameraRoadmap.body: A hidden camera can capture only the plant zone at fixed times for high-value clients that approve it.

cameraRoadmap.label: Plant-only capture
cameraRoadmap.body: Crop to the wall area, avoid people, blur accidental faces and keep photos scoped to maintenance evidence.

cameraRoadmap.label: Trend over time
cameraRoadmap.body: Daily or weekly visual trend data can improve early warnings, but it should not be needed for MVP.

qualityControls.label: Photo quality gate
qualityControls.rule: Reject blurry, overexposed, too-dark or wrong-angle photos before AI scoring.

qualityControls.label: Confidence threshold
qualityControls.rule: Low-confidence AI findings become review tasks instead of direct score changes.

qualityControls.label: Explainable score
qualityControls.rule: Every score must show top drivers such as low light, water swing, yellowing, open work order or recovery trend.

qualityControls.label: Client-safe reporting
qualityControls.rule: Reports show evidence-backed health status, not medical, air-purification or certification claims.

bands.label: Healthy
bands.action: Keep planned cadence and include in client proof.

bands.label: Watch
bands.action: Check photos, sensor trend and next visit timing.

bands.label: Recovery
bands.action: Create recovery work order, approve replacement plan and capture proof.

bands.label: Critical
bands.action: Escalate same-day FM response and account communication.
